title: trifolium longipes var. multiovulatum
intro: Trifolium longipes var. multiovulatum, also known as the longstalk clover, is a variety of perennial herb that belongs to the pea family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating digestive issues and as a diuretic. The plant's leaves and flowers are rich in flavonoids and saponins, which provide potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, Trifolium longipes var. multiovulatum is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can be consumed fresh or dried for its medicinal properties.
